steps.title: Use the symmetry line
steps.content: The vertex is $(-2,5)$, so the axis of symmetry is the vertical line $x=-2$. The two $x$-intercepts are the same horizontal distance from this line.

steps.title: Mirror the known intercept
steps.content:
Let $r$ be the other $x$-intercept. The midpoint of the two intercepts' $x$-coordinates is $-2$.
$$\frac{-\frac{15}{4}+r}{2}=-2$$
$$-\frac{15}{4}+r=-4$$
$$r=-\frac{1}{4}$$

steps.title: Write the other intercept
steps.content: The other $x$-intercept is $(-\frac{1}{4},0)$. The answer is B.
